nodes.label: Hard Rule #3: Audit logs required for all admin actions
nodes.norm_label: hard rule #3: audit logs required for all admin actions

nodes.label: BrandMember
nodes.description: Role-bearing member of a brand (OWNER / ADMIN / MEMBER).

nodes.label: KYB
nodes.description: Know Your Business verification — state machine NOT_STARTED -> PENDING -> APPROVED/REJECTED. Gates outbound payouts.

nodes.label: Invitation
nodes.description: Per-handle bounty invitation for CLOSED bounties. Status: PENDING / ACCEPTED / DECLINED / EXPIRED.

nodes.label: Application
nodes.description: Hunter application for CLOSED bounty access. PENDING -> APPROVED / REJECTED.

nodes.label: ReviewCenter
nodes.description: Cross-bounty submission review queue for a brand. Preferred entry point for reviewers.

nodes.label: DRAFT->LIVE Funding Gate
nodes.description: Bounty cannot flip to LIVE until paymentStatus = PAID via Stitch hosted checkout (POST /bounties/:id/fund). See CLAUDE.md §4.

nodes.label: Stitch Hosted Checkout
nodes.description: External redirect-based payment flow. Production uses window.location; dev/preview uses window.open(_blank). Encapsulated in redirect-to-hosted-checkout.ts.

nodes.label: AuditLog (Hard Rule #3)
nodes.description: Mandatory audit log entry on every admin action and status change.

nodes.label: AuthGuard business/layout
nodes.description: All business/* pages gated via AuthGuard allowedRoles={[BUSINESS_ADMIN]} at apps/web/src/app/business/layout.tsx.

nodes.label: switchBrand (multi-brand JWT)
nodes.description: POST /auth/switch-brand re-mints the JWT with the new brand-id claim. Used by my-brands and auto-triggered after create.

nodes.label: ConfirmAction (Hard Rule #6)
nodes.description: Destructive or status-changing actions wrapped in ConfirmAction dialog — universal for status transitions, deletes, escalations, upgrades, cancels.

nodes.label: VerificationReportPanel
nodes.description: Per-URL scrape verification surface with preview + submission modes. Used on bounty detail (preview) and submission review pages.

attributes.notes: Form is simulated — no real endpoint wired

attributes.purpose: Upgrade signed-in participant to BUSINESS_ADMIN by creating brand

attributes.notes: MOCK_PROFILE fallback dev-scaffolding, Invite to Bounty unwired

attributes.purpose: Directory for brands/admins to browse hunters

attributes.notes: Stale FAQ copy mentions Stripe (now Stitch Express)
Live upgrade via Stitch hosted card-consent

attributes.description: Hunter role; browses bounties, submits proof, files disputes
Brand-side admin; creates bounties, reviews submissions
Hunter's proof submission against a bounty
Participant-filed dispute against an approved submission
Hunter-focused participant wallet view aggregating ledger projection, balance cards, and recent transactions.

attributes.status: PAYOUTS_ENABLED=false (out of scope for live integration)

attributes.sections: acceptance, eligibility, account-registration, bounty-participation, payments-rewards, content-ownership, prohibited-conduct, dispute-resolution, limitation-of-liability, modifications, contact

nodes.label: claude.md
nodes.description: Project charter — Social Bounty MVP. Contains Hard Rules, Tech Stack, Agent Team roster, Financial Non-Negotiables, and the Knowledge Base & Financial Integrity Framework.

nodes.label: Social Bounty MVP
nodes.description: Bounty-based marketplace platform where businesses create tasks with rewards, users complete them and submit proof, businesses review submissions, and super admins manage the platform.

nodes.label: Hard Rules
nodes.description: Eight non-negotiable rules: MVP-only, RBAC-everywhere, Audit logs required, 100% test pass, PrimeReact+Tailwind only, confirmation dialogs for destructive actions, document assumptions, use agents for every task.

nodes.label: RBAC (Role-Based Access Control)
nodes.description: Mandatory on every screen and API endpoint. Part of Hard Rule #2.

nodes.label: AuditLog
nodes.description: Audit logs required for all admin actions and status changes. Accompanies every ledger write. Part of Hard Rule #3 and Financial Non-Negotiable #6.

nodes.label: Participant
nodes.description: User role. Browses bounties, submits proof, tracks submissions.

nodes.label: Business Admin
nodes.description: User role. Creates/manages bounties, reviews submissions, manages payouts.

nodes.label: Super Admin
nodes.description: User role. Full platform access, user/org management, audit logs, troubleshooting.

nodes.label: User (data model)
nodes.description: Core user entity.

nodes.label: Brand (data model)
nodes.description: Organisation was renamed to Brand in commit 539467e (2026-04-15).

nodes.label: Bounty (data model)
nodes.description: Core bounty entity with extended fields: channels, engagementRequirements, postVisibilityRule, structuredEligibility, accessType, etc.

nodes.label: Submission (data model)
nodes.description: Hunter submission with urlScrapes, consecutiveVisibilityFailures, approvedAt, lastVisibilityCheckAt tracking.

nodes.label: LedgerEntry (data model)
nodes.description: Append-only ledger entry; double-entry bookkeeping with integer minor units.

nodes.label: LedgerTransactionGroup (data model)
nodes.description: Groups ledger entries; UNIQUE(referenceId, actionType) provides idempotency.

nodes.label: WebhookEvent (data model)
nodes.description: Replay-safe webhook events; UNIQUE(provider, externalEventId).

nodes.label: StitchPaymentLink (data model)
nodes.description: Stitch hosted-checkout payment link record.

nodes.label: StitchPayout (data model)
nodes.description: Stitch/TradeSafe payout record. Gained a `provider` column (PayoutRail enum) in migration 20260418010000.

nodes.label: SystemSetting (data model)
nodes.description: DB-authoritative store for operational flags including `financial.kill_switch.active`.

nodes.label: Agent Team Lead
nodes.description: Approval gates, RBAC sign-off, financial-non-negotiable enforcement. Playbook: md-files/agents/agent-team-lead.md

nodes.label: Agent Architect
nodes.description: Database schema, API contracts, system design decisions, ADRs. Playbook: md-files/agents/agent-architect.md

nodes.label: Agent UX Designer
nodes.description: Flows, edge cases, destructive-action confirmation copy.

nodes.label: Agent UI Designer
nodes.description: PrimeReact composition, Tailwind tokens, screen inventory.

nodes.label: Agent Front-End
nodes.description: Next.js App Router, TanStack Query, routing, state.

nodes.label: Agent Back-End
nodes.description: NestJS modules, Prisma, auth/RBAC, ledger writers, webhook handlers.

nodes.label: Agent QA/Testing
nodes.description: 5-test matrix per ledger handler, Playwright E2E, smoke gates.

nodes.label: Agent DevOps
nodes.description: CI/CD, migrations, env validation, Docker/deployment.

nodes.label: Financial Non-Negotiables
nodes.description: Ten non-negotiable rules for every financial mutation: double-entry, idempotency, transaction group integrity, integer minor units, append-only ledger, AuditLog required, retry-safe, platform custody, plan snapshot, global fee independence.

nodes.label: Double-entry bookkeeping
nodes.description: Debits equal credits within each transaction.

nodes.label: Idempotency (UNIQUE referenceId, actionType)
nodes.description: Enforced at DB level via UNIQUE(referenceId, actionType).

nodes.label: Transaction group integrity
nodes.description: All steps commit or roll back together (single DB transaction or saga with compensation).

nodes.label: Integer minor units
nodes.description: Amounts stored as integers (cents), never floats.

nodes.label: AuditLog entry required
nodes.description: Required for every mutation (ties to Hard Rule #3).

nodes.label: Retry-safe
nodes.description: Handlers must tolerate timeouts, retries, and webhook replays.

nodes.label: Platform custody
nodes.description: All funds flow through platform-controlled custody. No direct brand-to-hunter payments.

nodes.label: Plan snapshot
nodes.description: Tier (Free/Pro) is snapshotted onto each transaction at creation time. In-flight transactions are never re-priced on plan change.

nodes.label: Global fee independence (3.5%)
nodes.description: Calculated independently of tier admin fee, stored in its own ledger account (global_fee_revenue), shown as a separate line.

nodes.label: TradeSafe
nodes.description: Outbound payout rail (per ADR 0008/0009). Adapter scaffolded, PAYOUTS_ENABLED=false gate.

nodes.label: LedgerService
nodes.description: Append-only ledger writer. isKillSwitchActive / setKillSwitch methods; postTransactionGroup with idempotency pre-check.

nodes.label: Reconciliation Engine
nodes.description: 7 checks running every 15min: balance, duplicate, reserve-vs-bounty, missing legs, status consistency, wallet projection drift, payouts-vs-ledger.

nodes.label: Finance Admin Dashboard
nodes.description: Kill switch, reconciliation drill-down, exception review, per-system confidence scores.

nodes.label: Svix Webhooks
nodes.description: Svix-verified HMAC-SHA256 webhooks with ≤5 min timestamp skew for Stitch integration.

nodes.label: md-files/payment-gateway.md
nodes.description: Canonical Stitch Express spec referenced from claude.md.

nodes.label: md-files/financial-architecture.md
nodes.description: Ledger mechanics, idempotency patterns, reconciliation engine spec.

nodes.label: md-files/knowledge-base.md
nodes.description: KB schema, entry template, known failure patterns.

nodes.label: md-files/admin-dashboard.md
nodes.description: Finance Reconciliation Dashboard spec, kill switch, KB insights.

nodes.label: md-files/implementation-phases.md
nodes.description: Phase 1–4 delivery plan (payments → reconciliation → dashboard → KB automation).

nodes.label: md-files/agents/agent-overview.md
nodes.description: Entry point for the 9 per-role agent specs.

nodes.label: Design System README
nodes.description: Canonical design system overview — visual foundations, tokens, components, iconography, shell navigation, voice. Claude Design handoff bundle 2026-04-18.

nodes.label: Design System SKILL.md
nodes.description: Short-form reference for agents building with the design system.

nodes.label: Design System ICONS.md
nodes.description: PrimeIcons → Lucide migration cheat sheet; 93 pi-* names mapped to Lucide equivalents.

nodes.label: colors_and_type.css
nodes.description: All design tokens (colors, type, radii, shadows, motion) + base typography.

nodes.label: components.css
nodes.description: Buttons, cards, inputs, badges, chips, avatars, tables, toasts, progress, shell-nav primitives.

nodes.label: --pink-600 (#db2777)
nodes.description: Brand color. Primary buttons, links, active states, focus rings, eyebrows. Hover = pink-700. Tint = pink-100.

nodes.label: --blue-600 (#2563eb)
nodes.description: Exists only as second stop of the signature gradient and the .info status color. Never standalone.

nodes.label: --slate-* tokens
nodes.description: Carries 80% of UI — text, borders, surfaces, dividers.

nodes.label: --reward-500 (gold)
nodes.description: Reserved for earnings, points, streaks. Never on interactive affordances.

nodes.label: Gradient once per view
nodes.description: linear-gradient(90deg, pink-600, blue-600) appears ONCE per view — on .gradient-text, progress bar, or marketing CTA pill.

nodes.label: Space Grotesk (headings)
nodes.description: Headings font. 400/500/600/700, -0.02em tracking at large sizes.

nodes.label: Inter (body)
nodes.description: Default body + labels font. 400/500/600.

nodes.label: JetBrains Mono (metrics)
nodes.description: Metrics, codes, IDs. 400/500, always tabular-nums.

nodes.label: .btn (button)
nodes.description: .btn + variants (-primary, -secondary, -ghost, -danger, -success, -cta), sizes (-sm -md -lg -xl).

nodes.label: .card
nodes.description: .card (16px radius, 24px padding), .card-interactive (hover lift), .card-feature (24px radius, 32px padding).

nodes.label: .input / .textarea / .select
nodes.description: Form inputs with pink focus ring. .input-error for invalid state.

nodes.label: .badge
nodes.description: Read-only status badge. Modifiers: -brand -success -warning -danger -info -neutral -reward.

nodes.label: .chip
nodes.description: Interactive counterpart to badge.

nodes.label: .avatar
nodes.description: .avatar with sizes -sm -lg -xl. .avatar-group overlaps with white ring.

nodes.label: .table
nodes.description: Slim borders, uppercase label headers, tabular-num cells.

nodes.label: .toast
nodes.description: .toast + status modifiers; 4px left accent, level-3 shadow.

nodes.label: .progress
nodes.description: Progress bar with pink→blue gradient.

nodes.label: Lucide Icons (lucide-react)
nodes.description: Canonical icon library. 20px or 24px, stroke-width:2, stroke:currentColor. Replaces PrimeIcons.

nodes.label: Shell Navigation
nodes.description: Nav is NavSection[] → NavItem[] → children. Active state = pink-50 bg + pink-700 text + 3px pink-600 left rail on expanded; pink-50 fill on collapsed.

nodes.label: previews/brand.html
nodes.description: Logo lockups, voice do/don't, imagery direction.

nodes.label: previews/colors.html
nodes.description: Every color ramp + in-context demos.

nodes.label: previews/components.html
nodes.description: Every component state preview.

nodes.label: previews/shell-navigation.html
nodes.description: Sidebar, collapsed rail, workspace switcher, mobile drawer canonical visual spec.

nodes.label: previews/spacing.html
nodes.description: Spacing scale, radii, shadows preview.

nodes.label: previews/type.html
nodes.description: Full type scale preview.

nodes.label: design-system/social-bounty/MASTER.md
nodes.description: Legacy generated design system master file (2026-03-31). Superseded by apps/web/src/styles/design-system/ canonical bundle.

nodes.label: BACKUP-STRATEGY.md
nodes.description: Backup and Disaster Recovery Strategy. Covers PostgreSQL, Redis, file uploads, RTO/RPO, monthly restore tests.

nodes.label: INCIDENT-RESPONSE.md
nodes.description: Incident Response Playbook v1.1. SEV-1 through SEV-4 severities, response timelines, §5.5 Financial Kill Switch procedure.

nodes.label: SECURITY-AUDIT.md
nodes.description: Sprint 1 security audit — npm dependency vulnerabilities, security headers, recommended upgrades.

nodes.label: SECURITY-COMPLIANCE.md
nodes.description: Security Compliance Audit Record — OWASP ASVS 4.0 reference. 17 areas audited (10 PASS, 5 PARTIAL, 2 FAIL).

nodes.label: STITCH-IMPLEMENTATION-STATUS.md
nodes.description: 5-minute orientation for Stitch Express. Inbound rail live, outbound gated via PAYOUTS_ENABLED=false.

nodes.label: contributing.md
nodes.description: Pre-commit/pre-push checks — npm run lint, npm test, npm run check:kill-switch-bypass.

nodes.label: apps/web/e2e/README.md
nodes.description: Playwright E2E tests guide. Chromium only. Tests hit live stack at localhost:3000 (web) + localhost:3001 (API).

nodes.label: create-bounty-spec.md
nodes.description: Create Bounty Product Specification — data model, business rules, state machine, API contract.

nodes.label: create-bounty-ui.md
nodes.description: Create Bounty UI Specification v1.1. PrimeReact component hierarchy, spacing, section specs, sticky footer.

nodes.label: create-bounty-ux.md
nodes.description: Create Bounty UX Flow & Interaction Design v1.0. 9 numbered sections, progressive disclosure, validation UX, reward line interactions.

nodes.label: create-bounty-tests.md
nodes.description: Create Bounty Validation Rules & Test Cases. Client-side + server-side rules, edge cases for every form field.

nodes.label: PAYOUTS_ENABLED=false gate
nodes.description: Outbound rail compile-and-route is active but inert. Flipping requires live TradeSafe creds (ADR 0008/0009).

nodes.label: Knowledge Base & Financial Integrity Framework
nodes.description: Operational framework extending Hard Rules: financial integrity protection, recurring-issue detection, Claude decision support before any fix.

nodes.label: ADR 0001 — Stripe Retirement Timing
nodes.summary: Replace Stripe with Stitch Express for inbound brand funding; phased flag-gated retirement with no hybrid long-term.

nodes.label: ADR 0002 — Wallet as Read-Model Projection over LedgerEntry
nodes.summary: Wallet becomes a projection over ledger; Withdrawal retires in favour of StitchPayout.
nodes.norm_label: adr 0002 — wallet as read-model projection over ledgerentry

nodes.label: ADR 0003 — TradeSafe Escrow Layer Out of Scope
nodes.summary: TradeSafe rejected as standalone escrow layer; platform reserve is the escrow. Payout-rail role reopened by ADR 0008.
nodes.norm_label: adr 0003 — tradesafe escrow layer out of scope

nodes.label: ADR 0004 — Feature Flag Inventory for Stitch Rollout
nodes.summary: Five flags: PAYMENTS_PROVIDER, PAYOUTS_ENABLED, RECONCILIATION_ENABLED, DASHBOARD_FINANCE_UI, KB_AUTO_STUB.
nodes.norm_label: adr 0004 — feature flag inventory for stitch rollout

nodes.label: ADR 0005 — Ledger Idempotency via Header Table
nodes.summary: UNIQUE(referenceId, actionType) lives on LedgerTransactionGroup header; individual LedgerEntry rows share transactionGroupId.
nodes.norm_label: adr 0005 — ledger idempotency via header table

nodes.label: ADR 0006 — Compensating Entries Bypass the Financial Kill Switch
nodes.summary: allowDuringKillSwitch:true only for actionType='compensating_entry' from two SUPER_ADMIN-gated callers.
nodes.norm_label: adr 0006 — compensating entries bypass the financial kill switch

nodes.label: ADR 0007 — Peach Payments for Hunter Payouts
nodes.summary: Peach chosen as outbound rail after Stitch no-beneficiary finding; superseded same day by ADR 0008.
nodes.norm_label: adr 0007 — peach payments for hunter payouts

nodes.label: ADR 0008 — TradeSafe for Hunter Payouts
nodes.summary: TradeSafe replaces Peach; hybrid model: Stitch inbound, TradeSafe outbound.

nodes.label: ADR 0009 — TradeSafe Integration Skeleton
nodes.summary: Architectural skeleton for TradeSafe: schema (Option B provider-agnostic), env vars, routing, webhook enum, clearance-window policy question.

nodes.label: ADR 0010 — Auto-Refund on PostVisibility Failure
nodes.summary: Two-failure threshold triggers automated refund via compensating ledger entry; kill-switch gated; per-bounty 30-rescrape cap.

nodes.label: REST API Contracts
nodes.summary: Canonical REST endpoint contract with JWT/RBAC conventions and pagination envelope.

nodes.summary: Prisma schema canonical doc covering entities, enums, indexes, cascade rules.

nodes.summary: Next.js App Router structure, route groups, state management.

nodes.label: Security and RBAC Design
nodes.summary: Role matrix, audit logging strategy, authentication flows, security checklist.

nodes.summary: Epics 1-10 for Social Bounty MVP with acceptance criteria.

nodes.summary: Production cutover gate list; cites ADRs 0001-0010 inclusive.

nodes.summary: Pre-flight checklist for TradeSafe outbound; reading order ADR 0008 → 0009 → this doc.

nodes.label: Agent Team Overview
nodes.summary: Entry point orchestration doc for 6-role + 2-reference agent system with strict sequential workflow UX -> UI -> Dev -> QA.

nodes.label: QA Testing Engineer Agent
nodes.summary: Final release gate. Jest unit + integration, Playwright E2E, 100% pass rate required. Runtime smoke test mandatory.

nodes.label: Prisma Migration Drafts README
nodes.summary: Explains packages/prisma/drafts/ holds non-auto-applied migration drafts; drafts must be promoted to timestamped migrations/ folders.

nodes.label: RBAC sign-off
nodes.summary: Ensuring every screen and API endpoint enforces role-based access control.

nodes.label: Financial non-negotiables enforcement
nodes.summary: Enforcing the double-entry, idempotency, audit, plan-snapshot and fee-independence rules on all ledger-touching PRs.

nodes.label: Approval gates enforcement
nodes.summary: Stop-and-approve scenarios: new components, new deps, new testing tools, new endpoints/architecture, scope changes.

nodes.label: Workflow sequencing enforcement
nodes.summary: Enforces UX -> UI -> Dev -> QA strict sequential workflow; no phase begins until the previous is complete.

nodes.label: Prisma schema ownership
nodes.summary: packages/prisma/schema.prisma; Prisma models, enums and indexes are architect-owned.

nodes.label: API contracts
nodes.summary: REST endpoint definitions under /api/v1 with request/response shapes, status codes and error format.

nodes.label: Architectural Decision Records
nodes.summary: Key architectural decisions (JWT auth, guard order, fire-and-forget audit, sanitize pipe, error format, soft delete).

nodes.label: Shared package contract
nodes.summary: @social-bounty/shared — enums, DTOs (~143), constants used by both web and api apps.

nodes.label: NestJS module implementation
nodes.summary: Controllers + services + DTOs + Prisma queries per feature module under apps/api/src/modules/.

nodes.label: Ledger writers
nodes.summary: Append-only double-entry ledger writes under LedgerService (referenced from claude.md financial non-negotiables).

nodes.label: Webhook handlers
nodes.summary: Svix-verified inbound webhooks (Stitch / TradeSafe) with replay-safe idempotency and audit logs.

nodes.label: Auth + RBAC guards
nodes.summary: JwtAuthGuard -> UserStatusGuard -> RolesGuard -> ThrottlerGuard chain registered as global APP_GUARDs.

nodes.label: Audit logging
nodes.summary: Fire-and-forget AuditService.log() for every mutation; errors logged but do not block response.

nodes.label: DTO input validation
nodes.summary: class-validator + global SanitizePipe + ValidationPipe (whitelist, forbidNonWhitelisted, transform).

nodes.label: Next.js App Router routing
nodes.summary: Route groups (auth)/(participant)/(shared) + real segments business/ admin/ to avoid route-group conflicts.

nodes.label: TanStack Query state
nodes.summary: Query client with staleTime 30s, gcTime 5m, retry 1; hierarchical queryKeys factory; custom hooks per domain.

nodes.label: PrimeReact component usage
nodes.summary: Approved PrimeReact 10 components only; severity-mapped buttons; common components (StatusBadge, PageHeader, etc.).

nodes.label: Auth context + guard
nodes.summary: Custom AuthContext (access in-memory, refresh in localStorage), AuthGuard wrapper, middleware role cookie gating.

nodes.label: Jest unit tests
nodes.summary: Service + guard unit tests with mocked Prisma/JWT/Audit/Mail services; Test.createTestingModule pattern.

nodes.label: 5-test matrix per ledger handler
nodes.summary: Happy path + retry idempotent + partial rollback + webhook replay + concurrent writer (referenced from claude.md).

nodes.label: Playwright E2E tests
nodes.summary: Browser-based smoke tests for critical user flows; CI job 4 runs on PRs only.

nodes.label: Runtime smoke testing
nodes.summary: Mandatory pre-release: start services, login each role, execute happy path, verify persistence and RBAC.

nodes.label: 100% pass rate gate
nodes.summary: Hard Rule #4: no release until all tests pass. Bugs returned to dev immediately with reproduction steps.

nodes.label: CI/CD pipeline
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions ci.yml: 4 jobs (lint-typecheck -> unit-tests -> integration-tests -> e2e-tests PRs only).

nodes.label: Prisma migrations workflow
nodes.summary: npx prisma migrate dev for local, migrate deploy for prod/CI. Schema at packages/prisma/schema.prisma.

nodes.label: Environment variable validation
nodes.summary: Typed env boot validation; JWT/DB/SMTP/Throttle/CORS/File-upload variables defined in .env.example.

nodes.label: Docker Compose for local dev
nodes.summary: PostgreSQL 16-alpine + MailHog for local dev stack.

nodes.label: Monorepo build order
nodes.summary: shared -> prisma generate -> api -> web; npm workspaces topology.

nodes.label: User flows definition
nodes.summary: Step-by-step journeys with decision points, error paths, success confirmation for each user role.

nodes.label: Edge-case identification
nodes.summary: Empty states, validation failure, session expiry, concurrent modification, role visibility edge cases.

nodes.label: Measurable success criteria
nodes.summary: Testable conditions that QA uses to validate a flow works (e.g., Participant can submit, BA cannot access other org).

nodes.label: Destructive-action confirmation copy
nodes.summary: Copy and flows for ConfirmAction / ConfirmDialog destructive-action gates (Hard Rule #6).

nodes.label: PrimeReact composition
nodes.summary: Visual designs composed only from the approved PrimeReact catalogue (lara-light-blue theme).

nodes.label: Tailwind design tokens
nodes.summary: Custom colour palette (primary/secondary/success/warning/danger/info/neutral) + layout/typography/spacing patterns.

nodes.label: UI audit before handoff
nodes.summary: Audit checklist: only approved components, only Tailwind utilities, palette-only colours, loading/error/empty states.

nodes.label: Screen + reusable component inventory
nodes.summary: Tracks existing reusable components (StatusBadge, PageHeader, BountyCard, etc.) to prevent duplicates.

nodes.label: Approval gates
nodes.summary: 5 stop-and-approve triggers: missing component, new dep, new testing tool, new endpoint/architecture, scope change.

nodes.label: Sequential workflow
nodes.summary: UX Designer -> UI Designer -> Development (FE + BE) -> QA Testing Engineer -> Release Gate.

nodes.label: Handoff points
nodes.summary: 7 handoffs documented: UX->UI, UI->FE, UI->BE, FE->QA, BE->QA, QA->Dev (bugs), QA->Team Lead (release).

nodes.label: Shared package as contract
nodes.summary: packages/shared/ is single source of truth for enums, DTOs, constants keeping FE + BE aligned at runtime.

nodes.label: Global guard chain
nodes.summary: JwtAuthGuard -> UserStatusGuard (60s TTL cache) -> RolesGuard -> ThrottlerGuard.

nodes.label: Monorepo structure
nodes.summary: apps/{api,web} + packages/{shared,prisma} wired via npm workspaces; build-order matters.

nodes.label: Prisma drafts convention
nodes.summary: Drafts live outside packages/prisma/migrations/ so prisma migrate deploy cannot auto-apply them; must be promoted to timestamped folders.

nodes.label: Social Bounty Wordmark
nodes.description: Canonical Social Bounty wordmark logo asset — PNG image showing the text 'Social Bounty' rendered in a bold, sans-serif typeface in a vivid pink color on a transparent background. The single-line, two-word lockup is the primary brand identity element in the design system.

nodes.label: Wordmark Typography Style
nodes.description: Heavy/bold weight sans-serif typeface with rounded, slightly geometric letterforms. Capital 'S' and 'B' lead the two words; generous letter spacing; friendly but assertive character. Conveys a modern, approachable brand voice consistent with the design system's 'pink leads' identity.

nodes.label: Wordmark Pink Fill
nodes.description: Solid vivid pink fill across all glyphs — visually matches the design system's pink-600 token (#db2777). No gradient, no secondary color, no outline. Single-color application reinforces pink as the lead brand color per the DS identity rule 'pink leads, blue counters'.

nodes.label: Wordmark Layout
nodes.description: Single-line horizontal two-word lockup: 'Social' on the left, 'Bounty' on the right, separated by a single space. Transparent background. No accompanying icon, symbol, or tagline — the wordmark stands alone as a text-only logo.

nodes.label: Wordmark Text: 'Social Bounty'
nodes.description: The literal text rendered in the image: 'Social Bounty' — the product name. Two words, title case.
